guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Neurology (acute headache red flags)
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: explosive severe headache with photophobia, flushing and brain fog mimics meningitis and subarachnoid haemorrhage.
Important-not-to-miss: new severe headache with confusion cannot have meningitis/SAH excluded remotely (no meningeal signs/fundoscopy over chat).
Anchoring: 'known mast cell flare' should not close off intracranial emergencies.
Commission trap: IM ketorolac/promethazine given in person do not translate to remote injections; NSAIDs already failed.
Telehealth limits: neurological examination, meningeal signs and fundoscopy cannot be performed over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
